title: Solo-session lane isolation — should a lone /next//prepare/resolve also run in an isolated lane clone?
summary: Prep research for decision #2123: a single-item session (a lone /next, /prepare, one-item resolve) today mutates the shared primary checkout in place; the parallel /workflow orchestrator (#1143/#1933) already isolates every unit of work in its own lane. The survey CORRECTS THE CARD'S PREMISE twice: (1) the constellation's isolation primitive is a lane CLONE, not a git worktree — the branch guard denies worktree-add in the shared checkout and the ratified #1996 statute (pr-flow-rollout-mechanism) codified 'isolation = a clone', so worktree-vs-clone is settled by precedent, not re-opened; (2) the go/no half is not a cold call — #1996 already ratified 'isolate-by-default for automation: every automated writing session works in a clone; main is convergence-only for automation', and its #1998 enforcement ladder names today's serial-on-main practice 'the interim'. So #2123 is the call that ENDS THE INTERIM for solo sessions — with one honest citation-scope caveat: #1996 weighed background automation racing live lanes, not the INTERACTIVE session's live-observation loop (the human watching :3000, conversational verify turns), so the interactive residual is re-derived on merit rather than inherited as settled. Grounding: the lane pool is built and proven (scripts/lane-pool.mjs, persistent --reference clones, per-lane npm ci; 2026-07-01 run 16/18 landed incl. cross-repo couples); per-lane dev-server ports are ratified and GENERATED (#1997 .env.local; vite/npm read the env with strictPort) but the chain has a skeptic-verified DEAD LINK — nothing sources .env.local into the shell/process env, so a lane npm run dev today boots 3000/8080 and strictPort collides loudly with the user's server; no lane has ever booted its dev pair for a Playwright before/after (the #1996 Gate-5 harness is unbuilt) — the honest not-yet on visual work; claims are structurally centralized (backlog.mjs claim guards via the open→active transition on visible shared state; the orchestrator pre-claims centrally and lanes never claim, #1933 choice 2) — a lane-local claim is invisible to every local-read consumer, grounding the claim-locus default; the landing path is already composed (commit on the lane's own main per Rule 104, push the durable lane/* ref, merge origin/main, authoritative gate on the MERGED tree per the gate-on-merged-tree anchor, land). External prior art: worktrees + per-instance ports are the industry norm for parallel coding agents (code isolation without runtime isolation — the same port caveat WE ratified); jj/Sapling workspaces make N-working-copies the first-class default posture; hosted harnesses (Devin/Copilot Workspace/Codex-style) offer ONLY the isolated-workspace-converge-via-PR model — the human-direct/automation-isolated asymmetry #1996 encoded. Recommended shape (post two skeptic passes + the fresh-context screen — a CONFIRM-AND-SHARPEN ruling, not a fresh four-element ratification): adoption = ACCEPTED-ON-MERIT, the go/no gate DISSOLVED per the merit-conceded rule (the screen flagged it prio: the go is ratified statute and every phase boundary is a mechanical capability edge — code-only solo builds lane now; visual + interactive-discussion sessions flip on the first green lane dev-pair probe; cross-repo solo lanes flip when the entry/closeout helper lands; no human weighing at flip time, the human turn compresses to a batch-confirm of the entry/landing mechanics); Fork 1 = claim locus, a genuine fork not a forced invariant (a remote-locus claim is coherent post never-push-removal) — default (a) claim on the primary, published as origin/main + the splice only (never primary HEAD, which would silently publish the human's unpushed commits), lanes never claim, resolve rides the lane's landing commit; Fork 2 = confirm-or-amend #1996's ratified 'every' clause — default confirm/uniform in steady state, interactive/content sessions sequenced behind the trigger (their lane-board mitigation cannot boot until the env-load link exists); Fork 3 = before-state soundness only (the verify-against-own-pair wiring is already #1996 statute) — default lane-local before, the primary's :3000 demoted to a provably-clean shortcut (per #1985's 'dirty primary is the normal baseline'). Recorded solo-lane costs: blindness to the user's uncommitted work (semantic-conflict residual) and no integrator layer over lane over-drops (re-verify before dropping). Full grounding in reports/2026-07-02-solo-session-worktree-lane-prior-art.md.
